name: Pentagon Hole Size
description: The 'Pentagon Hole Size' refers to a cluster of conspiracy theories surrounding the damage to the Pentagon during the September 11, 2001 attacks. These theories often question whether the observed damage was consistent with a Boeing 757 aircraft (American Airlines Flight 77) impacting the building. Proponents argue that the initial hole in the Pentagon's facade was too small to have been caused by a plane of that size, citing photographic and video evidence as well as eyewitness testimonies which they interpret as contradicting the official narrative. Some theories propose that the Pentagon was struck by a missile or drone, or that the event was a controlled demolition. The origin of these theories can be traced back to online forums and independent investigations that emerged shortly after the attacks. Mainstream analysis, including reports by the 9/11 Commission, engineering studies by the American Society of Civil Engineers, and analyses of recovered wreckage, concludes that the damage was indeed consistent with the impact of a Boeing 757. These analyses account for the aircraft's speed, angle of impact, and the subsequent collapse of the weakened structure. Critics of the conspiracy theories point to the lack of credible evidence supporting alternative explanations and the abundance of evidence supporting the official account, including recovered flight recorder data, DNA evidence, and the testimony of numerous first responders and eyewitnesses who reported seeing the plane impact the building. Furthermore, the spread of misinformation and the misinterpretation of technical data contribute to the persistence of these theories.
subtitle: 9/11 Pentagon impact controversy

claims.claim: The initial hole in the Pentagon was too small for a Boeing 757.
claims.evidence: Photographs and videos of the Pentagon immediately after the attack show a relatively small hole.
claims.criticism: The initial hole was only the point of entry. The aircraft’s fuselage disintegrated as it entered the building, and the subsequent collapse of the weakened structure created a larger opening. Internal damage extended much further than the entry point.

claims.claim: No plane debris was found at the scene.
claims.evidence: Some claim that there was a lack of identifiable plane wreckage at the Pentagon crash site.
claims.criticism: Significant amounts of aircraft debris, including engine parts, landing gear components, and sections of the fuselage, were recovered from the site and positively identified as belonging to a Boeing 757. DNA evidence also confirmed the presence of passengers and crew.

claims.claim: The damage is more consistent with a missile strike.
claims.evidence: The nature of the damage and the lack of visible plane parts are cited as evidence of a missile strike.
claims.criticism: There is no credible evidence to support a missile strike. The recovered wreckage, flight recorder data, and eyewitness accounts all contradict this theory. The damage patterns are consistent with the known characteristics of a high-speed aircraft impact.

keyFigures.name: Thierry Meyssan
keyFigures.role: Author of '9/11: The Big Lie', a key text promoting the Pentagon attack conspiracy.

keyFigures.name: David Ray Griffin
keyFigures.role: Author and philosopher who has written extensively promoting 9/11 conspiracy theories.

keyFigures.name: Dylan Avery
keyFigures.role: Director of the 'Loose Change' documentary series, which promotes 9/11 conspiracy theories.

keyFigures.name: The 9/11 Commission
keyFigures.role: Official commission that investigated the 9/11 attacks and concluded that a Boeing 757 struck the Pentagon.

timeline.event: American Airlines Flight 77 crashes into the Pentagon.
Initial conspiracy theories regarding the Pentagon attack begin to circulate online.
Thierry Meyssan publishes '9/11: The Big Lie'.
The 'Loose Change' documentary is released, popularizing 9/11 conspiracy theories.
The 9/11 Commission Report is published, concluding that Flight 77 struck the Pentagon.

sources.author: David Ray Griffin
sources.work: The New Pearl Harbor: Disturbing Questions About the Bush Administration and 9/11
sources.title: David Ray Griffin
